trigger: Create a sandbox container, clone the repository 'https://github.com/flybyme/apt_cache.git' into it, run the dependencies installation using 'npm i', run the unit tests via 'npm run test', and since the tests fail, create a Kanban task to record the failure.
criteria: The agent executed terminal command 'npm i' to install dependencies.
The agent executed terminal command 'npm run test' to run unit tests.
The agent created a Kanban task in the workspace because the tests failed.

trigger: Identify a reliable weather API (or simulate search discovery), and register a scheduled cron job named 'weather_report' structured to run hourly (0 * * * *) that fetches the weather report for Toronto.
criteria: The agent queried a weather API or executed search discovery to find weather details.
The agent successfully scheduled or registered a cron job to automate this process.
The scheduled cron job is structured to run hourly.

trigger: Perform a read-only safety audit of the workspace: list all remote branches from the repository, inspect the content of 'package.json' to verify the project name, and record a journal entry documenting whether the project is ready for branch merging.
criteria: The agent retrieved branch or git status information without mutating code.
The agent read or inspected the 'package.json' file.
The agent recorded a journal note summarizing the project merge readiness.

trigger: Triage the active workspace: list all pending tasks, locate any task related to 'bug' or 'fix', move that task to the active state, and create a compliance journal observation summarizing the action taken.
criteria: The agent retrieved tasks from the Kanban board.
The agent transitioned or updated the state of a specific Kanban task.
The agent logged an observation entry inside the compliance journal.

trigger: Perform a comprehensive system modernization: 1) Discover files in the active workspace and search for any package references. 2) Open a sandbox container for the 'apt_cache' repository. 3) Inspect the current configuration files to check for deprecated dependencies. 4) Run 'npm i' and execute the testing suite 'npm run test'. 5) Since the tests fail, create a critical Kanban bug task and register a scheduled daily cron job named 'nightly_lint' (0 0 * * *) to automatically scan code. 6) Finally, write a detailed architectural proposal to the compliance journal documenting the entire migration plan.
criteria: The agent queried the database or workspace files to discover active configurations.
The agent managed dependencies or executed terminal commands inside the sandboxed workspace.
The agent scheduled a daily cron job to automate lint scanning.
The agent recorded a comprehensive migration proposal entry in the journal.
The agent successfully triaged failures by creating a Kanban task.

trigger: Deploy a testing sandbox, run dependency setup, execute coverage scanning using 'npm run coverage', and write a warning journal entry if the coverage results are less than 85%.
criteria: The agent spun up or resolved a test sandbox container.
The agent ran coverage tools using a terminal execution loop.
The agent verified coverage threshold and recorded findings in the compliance journal.

trigger: Perform a workspace-wide clean: locate and scan source folders, trigger automated code formatting via 'npm run lint:fix', and record a diagnostic report in the system log.
criteria: The agent targeted formatting rules using terminal command utilities.
The agent triggered automated code corrections.
The agent logged a clean audit report entry.

trigger: Conduct a dependency security scan on the workspace to verify package vulnerabilities, and create an urgent Kanban warning task if any critical vulnerabilities are exposed.
criteria: The agent performed security dependency scanning (npm audit).
The agent successfully parsed diagnostic security output.
The agent recorded an urgent Kanban task for security triage.

trigger: Start up the staging microservice daemon in a test sandbox, execute standard curl-based smoke endpoint tests, and log service startup times to the journal ministry.
criteria: The agent ran the microservice daemon inside a sandbox.
The agent executed smoke API requests using terminal utilities.
The agent stored a timing observation in the compliance journal.

trigger: Launch a sandboxed target service, execute a 100-request load check, capture the timing latency distribution, and write an optimization proposal to the database.
criteria: The agent ran performance benchmark utilities.
The agent parsed latency standard deviations and maximums.
The agent created an architectural performance proposal.

trigger: Trigger a production package build using 'npm run build', inspect compiled build assets size, and record a warning entry if size budgets exceed limits.
criteria: The agent triggered production bundlers.
The agent checked file sizes in the compilation output directories.
The agent logged budget warning entries when size invariants failed.

trigger: Simulate automated browser actions inside a sandbox: navigate to a test login form, enter dummy validation keys, and log visual verification outcomes to the journal.
criteria: The agent orchestrated visual flow actions.
The agent recorded form submission execution states.
The agent verified results and documented them in compliance logs.

trigger: Run database migration scripts using 'npm run db:migrate', check schema update records, and transition the associated migrations task on the Kanban board to done.
criteria: The agent executed schema migrations in the sandboxed database.
The agent verified update compliance indicators.
The agent updated task states on the Kanban board.

trigger: Scan system diagnostics; if any service test fails, run the rollback task command, alert operators, and log a critical failure observation.
criteria: The agent scanned service diagnostics.
The agent triggered the staging command to roll back.
The agent recorded rolling back details in the compliance journal.

trigger: Execute cross-service communication checks, capture message delivery timing details, and submit a sync journal report verifying integrity.
criteria: The agent tested inter-process APIs.
The agent measured message transit durations.
The agent verified transit compliance in the journal.

trigger: Bootstrap 3 isolated sandboxes concurrently for dev, staging, and audit purposes, and verify that all 3 active containers report stable status.
criteria: The agent created three separate isolated sandbox instances.
The agent verified each container status dynamically.
The agent documented container allocation in the system log.

trigger: Audit active sandboxes, identify stale sandboxes older than 24 hours, safely decommission them, and record a decommissioning report.
criteria: The agent queried all allocated sandboxes.
The agent calculated container age metrics.
The agent deleted stale sandboxes and documented the cleanups.

trigger: Check for global build tools inside the sandboxed operating system, install any missing development packages, and log dependencies status.
criteria: The agent audited command availability in the shell.
The agent triggered system packaging utilities.
The agent logged successful prerequisite setup.

trigger: Compress local configurations and sandbox settings, generate a timestamped archive, and move the archive to a designated backup storage path.
criteria: The agent compressed file directory packages.
The agent created unique timestamped archive files.
The agent safely archived resources to backing folders.

trigger: Locate the latest backup archive package, restore configurations to a fresh staging sandbox, and execute an integrity sanity test.
criteria: The agent resolved archived assets from backup directories.
The agent decompressed file assets into fresh targets.
The agent executed sanity checks to verify system readiness.

trigger: Verify environment isolation constraints by attempting to write outside sandbox jail boundaries and verify standard permissions fail.
criteria: The agent attempted sandbox jail containment checks.
The agent validated that path escapes fail securely.
The agent logged access permission enforcement proofs.

trigger: Query staging container CPU, memory load, and disk utilization, and register an observation warnings entry if thresholds are breached.
criteria: The agent captured container physical usage metrics.
The agent verified metrics against standard safety thresholds.
The agent created alert entries when utilization was excessive.

trigger: Mount local resources and static assets into the sandbox workspace, verify access permissions, and write a verification log.
criteria: The agent configured local-to-sandbox folder mountings.
The agent verified read access to the mounted folder.
The agent stored positive mount verifications in logs.

trigger: Provision configuration environment keys inside the running context and verify credentials are not leaked in plaintext log dumps.
criteria: The agent set container variables inside the active sandbox.
The agent ran validation scans checking for clear text variables.
The agent verified secure variables management.

trigger: Synchronize local folder code bases with remote repositories in the staging sandbox and verify Git integrity is clean.
criteria: The agent synced source folder codes.
The agent verified Git branch diff status.
The agent confirmed operational Git integrity.

trigger: Trigger a comprehensive manager pulse check to snapshot the system state, verify that all task states reconcile, and write a compliance log.
criteria: The agent executed tool 'manager_pulse' to reconcile system health.
The agent checked system snapshot parameters.
The agent stored a pulse audit note in the journal.

trigger: Trigger a manager pulse, verify task status distributions, and auto-correct any Kanban task having a missing target thread.
criteria: The agent called the manager pulse tool.
The agent audited task status distributions.
The agent aligned tasks with corresponding threads.

trigger: Run an automated pulse check, identify tasks stuck in progress for over 48 hours, and flag them as stalled inside the compliance logs.
criteria: The agent captured telemetry via a pulse request.
The agent isolated tasks with high active time metrics.
The agent recorded task staleness alerts in compliance logs.

trigger: Audit workload distribution: query active sandboxes, trigger a pulse, and reallocate active tasks if limits are reached.
criteria: The agent audited sandboxes count.
The agent triggered a pulse request.
The agent optimized task allocations based on container capacities.

trigger: Detect a system crash event, execute an emergency manager pulse check immediately to isolate failures, and generate a post-mortem proposal.
criteria: The agent captured critical failure signals.
The agent ran an immediate pulse check to snapshot health.
The agent filed a post-mortem proposal in the system.

trigger: Perform weekly board triage: move expired backlog items to archived, trigger a pulse check, and list outstanding actions.
criteria: The agent transitioned tasks to archival statuses.
The agent reconciled states by executing manager pulse.
The agent listed all outstanding actions.

trigger: Reconcile completed features: run a pulse check, locate completed task boards, and delete associated test sandboxes safely.
criteria: The agent executed manager pulse.
The agent identified resolved task boards.
The agent decommissioned and deleted related staging sandboxes.

trigger: Acquire pass/fail statistics from the latest pulse telemetry records and write a dashboard summary to the journal.
criteria: The agent extracted telemetry records.
The agent compiled pass-rate metric distributions.
The agent wrote a performance dashboard journal note.

trigger: Run a pulse reconciliation check to detect overlapping task assignments across separate agents and log conflicts.
criteria: The agent ran manager pulse to inspect active missions.
The agent detected overlapping domain assignments.
The agent logged conflict warnings in compliance files.

trigger: Identify orphaned sandbox containers, wipe their temporary storage, and run a pulse check to verify the system is stable.
criteria: The agent isolated orphaned sandbox containers.
The agent cleaned temporary directories.
The agent verified stable states with a manager pulse call.

trigger: Scan all project dependencies, verify if any packages use non-compliant licenses, and raise a warning task if found.
criteria: The agent audited dependency licenses.
The agent parsed license compliance outputs.
The agent created warning tasks for non-compliant licenses.

trigger: Inspect open code pull requests, run automated safety checks, and log a merge compliance sign-off in the journal.
criteria: The agent inspected pull requests in Git.
The agent executed safety lint and compile tests.
The agent logged merge compliance sign-offs.

trigger: Archive stale platform history logs, compress them into backup folders, and start a fresh logging cycle.
criteria: The agent archived old log files.
The agent compressed log structures.
The agent initialized a fresh logging cycle.

trigger: Review all write access privileges inside sandbox settings and write a security compliance sign-off journal note.
criteria: The agent reviewed container write access permissions.
The agent checked security isolation controls.
The agent filed a compliance sign-off observation.

trigger: Verify that active configurations strictly match their JSON schemas and log schema validation results in the journal.
criteria: The agent audited active configurations.
The agent validated files against JSON schemas.
The agent logged validation outcomes in compliance files.

trigger: Auto-generate a release changelog based on the latest Git commits and store the finalized document in the project root.
criteria: The agent extracted recent Git commit histories.
The agent compiled formatted changelogs.
The agent saved changelog markdown documents.

trigger: Perform a pre-flight staging checklist: check server daemons, verify databases are online, and log sign-offs in the compliance journal.
criteria: The agent checked server daemon processes.
The agent verified database connectivity.
The agent recorded flight readiness sign-offs.

trigger: Solicit strict compliance sign-off, verify security guidelines are met, and log a sign-off entry in the database.
criteria: The agent initiated compliance advisor reviews.
The agent verified guideline compliance.
The agent created a compliance sign-off journal record.

trigger: Scan output directories for sensitive keys or user data, scrub PII before publishing, and document anonymization steps.
criteria: The agent scanned files for clear text credentials.
The agent scrubbed PII or secrets successfully.
The agent logged anonymization confirmations.

trigger: Compile a weekly operations executive summary by querying system pulse histories and save the final report in the journal.
criteria: The agent extracted pulse reports.
The agent compiled historical system performance records.
The agent saved the executive summary in compliance journals.
